summary: Frontend-only verification of /services/cinema/booking/{showtimeId} after the 'Black Panther mock removal' change. Both flows verified: (a) direct deep-link with NO sessionStorage, and (b) navigation from /services/cinema/film/{id} -> click ShowtimeCard. In both cases the booking page renders the selected film/cinema/date/time (Olympus has Fallen - EN @ PH-Netflix, Wed, Jul 1, 2026, 15:00, VIP). The Black Panther mock string is absent (count=0). Poster URL is correctly prefixed via resolvePoster() to '<origin>/api/static/films/<id>.png' and renders in the order-summary header. Step indicator, tickets +/-, payment-method card and Confirm-booking CTA are all still present and unchanged.

verified_observations.header_hero_meta_row: MapPin PH-Netflix | Calendar Wed, Jul 1, 2026 | Clock 15:00 | VIP badge - all rendered with the selected film name 'Olympus has Fallen - EN'

regression_checks.seat_map: Rendered with rows A and B, legend (Available/Selected/VIP/Booked/Blocked)

critical_code_review_comments: loadData() correctly merges sessionStorage prime then API authoritative refresh; on full API failure with no prime it nulls out state instead of falling back to mock - good.

resolvePoster() only prefixes '/api/' paths; relative paths starting with '/static/...' or other absolute roots would remain un-prefixed. Not an issue with current backend which always serves '/api/static/...'.
Header h1 inside the booking content shows the actual selected film title; the page document body still includes a global sidebar 'Welcome back' heading which is expected layout (not a regression).

If both sessionStorage prime is missing AND the API request fails, the page renders an empty-ish UI (no title, no cinema, no date). Consider showing an explicit error state with retry/back navigation.
seed_data_creation: None - used existing seeded showtime id c58f7a3e-6586-4606-b3cb-15cf0f23f71d (Olympus has Fallen - EN @ PH-Netflix, 2026-07-01 15:00, VIP).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both code paths (sessionStorage-primed via ShowtimeCard click, and cold deep-link) now correctly render the selected film/cinema/date/time on /services/cinema/booking/{id}. No Black Panther mock anywhere. Poster src resolved through resolvePoster() to absolute origin + /api/static/...; image loads. Login flow on this app requires clicking 'Login to Your Account' first to reveal the email/password form (two-step login screen). Payment was NOT submitted as instructed.
